steps.title: Signal the contrast
steps.content: The passage first gives a sleek, streamlined jet as an example of aerodynamics. The next sentence turns to a boxy semitruck that encounters more wind resistance, so the transition must signal a contrast between the two examples.

steps.title: Reject the wrong relations
steps.content: A suggests cause and effect, but the truck's resistance is not caused by the jet. B would introduce a more specific example of the same idea, and C would conclude the discussion. The sentence needs a transition that marks the opposite case. The answer is D.
